Title: Veeam backups were failed for ISI
Notes: Investigated recurring ESXi login/backup failures on host 192.168.1.4 (“Cannot complete login…”). Gained console access, enabled SSH, and reviewed /var/log/auth.log and hostd.log; observed repeated esx.audit.account.locked events (52–58 failed attempts per burst) and traced sources to 192.168.1.20 (Veeam server) and 192.168.1.12 (unidentified). On the Veeam v12.3 server, consolidated to a single “ESXi Credentials” entry, repointed Managed Servers → VMware vSphere → 192.168.1.4 to the new credential, and rescanned; reviewed Service Providers and noted legacy credentials (Provider/Tenant Network Extension & Azure helper) still present pending detachment. Verified NTP on ESXi is running and time is in sync; confirmed the host isn’t joined to vCenter (vpxa stopped). Implemented hardening by restricting ESXi firewall (SSH/HTTPS) to Veeam and admin IPs; disabled SSH after validation. Cleaned up DNS by identifying VMIS2 → 192.168.1.12 as a stale A record and set monitoring to capture MAC/host if .12 reappears. Final check: Veeam rescan succeeded and ESXi logs showed only successful root authentications from 192.168.1.20 with no new lockouts.

Title: Veeam backups were failed for ISI
Notes:
Today, I continued troubleshooting the recurring ESXi backup job failures caused by authentication issues and possible SSH connection anomalies. Upon initial inspection, the ESXi host (192.168.1.4) was again inaccessible via the Veeam job, showing the same login error as before. Verified that SSH was responsive, but the web interface login for root continued to fail.

Checked the VBR server health — it was observed that the system was sluggish and some backup-related services were unresponsive. Rebooted the ISI-HQ-VBR-01 server to clear any hanging Veeam or management processes. After reboot, performed a chkdsk to verify disk integrity and ran a System File Checker (sfc /scannow) to rule out OS-level corruption. Both completed successfully with no major issues detected.

Despite these actions, the Veeam backup job still failed with the same credential/authentication issue against the ESXi host. The problem remains under observation; SSH logs will continue to be monitored overnight for new activity or brute-force patterns from any internal IPs (especially 192.168.1.12).

Title: Veeam backups were failed for ISI
Notes: Investigated recurring Veeam backup failures caused by ESXi root authentication lockouts. Found repeated failed login attempts from 192.168.1.20 (VBR server) in the ESXi /var/log/auth.log, which triggered the PAM lockout mechanism. Connected to the ESXi host via SSH, verified the lock status using pam_tally2 --user root, and cleared it with pam_tally2 --user root --reset, confirming zero failures. Modified the PAM configuration file /etc/pam.d/system-auth to set unlock_time=600 for a 10-minute automatic unlock period. After resetting the lock and ensuring the correct credentials were used in Veeam, the backup jobs ran successfully without further authentication errors. Monitoring will continue to confirm long-term stability and prevent future lockouts.

Title: Veeam backups were failed for ISI
Notes: Today I continued troubleshooting the recurring ESXi root account lockouts and Veeam Backup & Replication service failures. I identified that the ESXi credentials were still stored in the Veeam ONE application, which was repeatedly attempting authentication and causing lockouts; the credential entry was removed. The VBR server was also failing to start Veeam services due to a missing PostgreSQL service, so I manually re-registered the PostgreSQL-x64-15 Windows service using pg_ctl, verified the data directory, and successfully restored the database service. Once PostgreSQL was online, all Veeam services started normally and the VBR console loaded successfully. I confirmed that the repository scan is now running and the environment is stable. The case will be handed over to the next shift to perform an ESXi rescan and run the backup job.

Title: ISI Backup did not Run automatically for Scheduled time
Notes: Veeam Backup & Replication is currently not operational due to an unsupported and mismatched version between the Veeam Console and the Backup Server; during troubleshooting it was confirmed that the core service Veeam Backup Service (VeeamBackupSvc) is missing, SQL dependencies are not present or not functioning, port 9392 is not listening, and multiple service start attempts failed with errors 1053/1060/1064, indicating an incomplete or failed upgrade rather than a permissions or command issue. As a result, the console cannot connect to 127.0.0.1, and the issue cannot be resolved by force-starting services; a full Veeam update or repair to a supported matching version is required to reinstall the missing core service. we will inform to lead regarding this to update the virsion to letest.

Title: ISI Backup did not Run automatically for Scheduled time
Notes: After discussion with the Lead, attempted to repair the Veeam Backup & Replication software, but the issue persisted. Then checked the installed drivers in APD and noticed that the PostgreSQL patch was missing on the server. Downloaded and repaired the PostgreSQL setup, which was found to be corrupted. After repairing PostgreSQL, rebooted the server. Post reboot, verified that all required services were running successfully. Logged into the Veeam Backup & Replication console and started the backup jobs. The backups are now running correctly.

Title: Finding the mac address for the Vmware Esxi.
Notes: There was an issue where the VMware Esxi was able access from only one computer and was not able to connect from other computers. Title: ISI backups were failed
Notes: Investigated intermittent and inconsistent access to ESXi host management interface. Identified root cause as an IP address conflict between the ESXi management interface (vmk0) and the Zyxel GS1900 switch, both configured with the same IP address. Remotely accessed the Zyxel switch, reassigned its management IP to a verified free address, and confirmed successful connectivity on the new IP. Cleared ARP conflicts and validated stable access to the ESXi host from multiple machines. Reviewed the network to confirm no additional IP conflicts, verified available IP addresses, and implemented DHCP/IP reservations for all virtual machines and critical network devices to prevent recurrence. Issue fully resolved and environment stabilized.

Title: ISI front desk is having the issue with Credit Card terminal
Notes: As per Rishad they are having the issue with Credit Card terminal Reviewed and troubleshot credit card terminal connectivity issues where the device functioned correctly when connected directly to the modem but failed when placed behind the Netgear R6400 router. Verified router configuration including SIP ALG, SPI firewall, DMZ, NAT behavior, and existing port forwarding rules. Confirmed the terminal was correctly configured using DHCP with an IP reservation. Identified inconsistent DNS configuration across the environment, with different systems receiving multiple DNS servers (including legacy and public DNS), which could impact secure outbound payment processing. Standardized the environment by confirming Windows DHCP service on ISI-HQ-AD-01 (192.168.1.27) as the sole DHCP authority and updating DHCP Scope Option 006 to provide only the Active Directory–integrated DNS server. Verified all servers, virtual machines, and workstations are now receiving the correct DHCP configuration, default gateway, and DNS settings. Validated DNS resolution from multiple endpoints. Environment is now in a stable and compliant state; pending end-user confirmation of successful payment transactions before removing temporary router workarounds (DMZ/port forwarding) and closing the issue.

Title: Server  restart after APD ISI-HQ-VBR-01 & ISI-HQ-VONE
Notes:
As per the handover received from the previous shift regarding the Veeam Backup service failing to start (which prevented access to the Veeam console), the issue was investigated. It was identified that the PostgreSQL patch was missing and the PostgreSQL installation on the server was corrupted.

The PostgreSQL setup was downloaded and repaired. After completing the repair, the server was rebooted. Post-reboot, all required services were verified and confirmed to be running successfully.

Logged into the Veeam Backup & Replication console and restarted the backup jobs. The backups are now passed successfully.

Title: ISI - Site was down
Notes:

Title: Veeam backups job failed for ISI
Notes: Monitor the Veeam backups for ISI , backup job were  failed today. So while connected to the server ISI-HQ-VBR-01 and found Veeam console was giving error of connection lost . So reboot the server, after that which indicated that the Veeam services were not running. I rebooted the server and attempted to manually start the services, but they still failed to start. I checked the system and found several missing Windows updates, so I installed all pending updates and rebooted again, but the services continued to fail. Since there was no repair option available in the existing installation, I logged into the Veeam portal, downloaded the latest Veeam Backup & Replication ISO, and attempted to run the installer. The installation failed with a PostgreSQL connection error, indicating that the PostgreSQL service was missing. I then downloaded and installed PostgreSQL manually on the server, restarted the Veeam installation, and allowed it to complete successfully. After the installation finished, I rebooted the server and verified that all Veeam services and the application were running properly without any further issues.

Title: veeam issue in ISI-HQ-VBR-
Notes:
As per the handover from the previous shift, it was noted that the SQL backup installation was pending on the ISI-HQ-VBR system. The following steps were followed to ensure proper installation and monitoring of the process:

  1. Monitoring Installation:
    The SQL backup installation was initiated and monitored closely. The setup for PostgreSQL was underway, with several files being unpacked and installed during the process.

  2. Installation Progress:
    During the installation, a Stack Builder Error was encountered, indicating that no package had been selected for installation. This was resolved by selecting the appropriate packages to proceed with the installation.

  3. Application Selection:
    Under the Stack Builder interface, the relevant category was selected (Add-ons, tools, and utilities) along with the necessary PostgreSQL add-ons, database drivers, and utilities.

  4. SQL Backup Setup:
    After resolving the issue, the installation continued smoothly. All required components were installed, and the SQL backup system was set up successfully.

  5. Current Status:
    The SQL backup installation is now complete, and the system is in a stable state. No further issues were encountered during the process.

  6. Recommendations for Future:
    It is recommended to verify the SQL backup configuration periodically and ensure that all necessary updates and patches are installed to prevent any further issues.
